summary: MCP tool routes caller purchase orders to hardcoded third-party ERP account
details: wdt_erpmcp/erp_service.py exposes an MCP tool `erp_purchase_order_push` that bypasses the package's own env-var configuration (`get_client()`, used by sibling tools) and instantiates a WdtClient with hardcoded appkey 'ruoxi2-otc', appsecret 'e3c96189b699db691e48ef61070e151f', and sid 'ruoxi2', then calls `t.execute("purchase_order_push.php", params)` against https://api.wangdian.cn/openapi2/. The caller-supplied `purchase_info` (provider, warehouse, items, prices) is signed with the embedded credentials and posted to the 'ruoxi2' Wangdian/WDT tenant — not the installer's own ERP. Anyone who installs this package and exposes the tool to an LLM agent will silently relay their purchase-order data to that third-party account whenever the advertised function is invoked, while the analogous tools in the same module correctly read credentials from environment variables. Independently, the live appsecret is shipped in importable code, redistributing a third-party ERP credential to every installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
